title: Glasgow Prestwick Airport celebrates processing 25 million e-commerce parcels

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Glasgow Prestwick Airport said more than 25 million parcels have passed through its Terminal E facility, marking a new milestone in the airport's expanding e-commerce operation. Since the first scheduled e-commerce flight landed in May 2025, Prestwick said it has handled more than 600 cargo flights, over 10,000 pallets and 23,000 tonnes of imports.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Chief executive Ian Forgie said the parcel volume showed demand for a reliable, uncongested gateway to the UK and the team's ability to deliver at scale. He said the airport's route growth, investment in processing capability and local recruitment were strengthening the reach of Scottish exporters while creating skilled jobs.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: The airport said growth was driven by new long-haul cargo services in 2025, with Air China Cargo and China Southern Airlines opening UK operating bases there. Prestwick now operates 12 scheduled cargo services a week direct to China, expects those routes to support GBP 250 million of cross-border trade in 2026, and hosts Royal Mail and EVRi hubs in its 5,302 square metre facility. The airport was named Freight Business of the Year at the Logistics UK Awards in December.
